CREATE TABLE timestamps (id INT, ts TIMESTAMPTZ);
INSERT INTO timestamps VALUES (1, TIMESTAMPTZ '1992-09-20 20:38:48'), (2, TIMESTAMPTZ '1992-03-22 01:02:03.1234'), (3, TIMESTAMPTZ '2000-01-01 00:00:00'), (4, TIMESTAMPTZ '2020-10-05 12:30:00');
CREATE TABLE timestamps2 (id INT, ts TIMESTAMPTZ);
INSERT INTO timestamps2 VALUES (1, TIMESTAMPTZ '1995-05-20 14:38:48'), (2, TIMESTAMPTZ '1995-01-01 23:59:59.9999');
SELECT least(t1.ts, t2.ts) FROM timestamps t1 JOIN timestamps2 t2 ON t1.id = t2.id;
SELECT least(least(TIMESTAMPTZ '1992-09-20 20:38:48', TIMESTAMPTZ '1992-03-22 01:02:03.1234'), TIMESTAMPTZ '2000-01-01 00:00:00') AS earliest_timestamp;
SELECT ts FROM timestamps WHERE ts = (SELECT least(ts) FROM timestamps);
SELECT TS, least(ts, TIMESTAMPTZ '2000-12-31 23:59:59') as comparison FROM timestamps WHERE ts < TIMESTAMPTZ '2021-01-01 00:00:00';
SELECT id, least(ts, TIMESTAMPTZ '1992-09-21 20:38:48') AS earliest FROM timestamps ORDER BY earliest;
SELECT id, least(TIMESTAMPTZ '1991-01-01 00:00:00', TIMESTAMPTZ '1991-02-01 00:00:00') AS earliest_timestamp FROM timestamps WHERE id IN (1, 2);
WITH temp AS (SELECT ts FROM timestamps WHERE ts IS NOT NULL) SELECT least(ts, TIMESTAMPTZ '2050-01-01 00:00:00') FROM temp;
